# CertifiedData.io — Public SDK & Verification Tools

> CertifiedData.io is a certificate authority for AI artifacts.
> It issues cryptographically signed certificates that prove a dataset was synthetically generated.

## What this repository contains

This is the public-facing SDK, verification tools, and documentation repository for CertifiedData.io.

It contains:
- TypeScript packages for verifying certificates and rendering trust badges
- Examples for Node.js, browser, CI pipelines, and Python
- OpenAPI spec for the REST API
- Canonical schema definitions and JSON Schemas
- Test fixtures for local verification

## Core packages

### @certifieddata/cli
Unified command-line entrypoint. Wraps @certifieddata/pii-scan and @certifieddata/verify
with a three-step developer flow: detect PII, hand off to generation, verify the result.

```bash
certifieddata pii-scan customers.csv --emit-handoff --output-handoff handoff.json
certifieddata generate --handoff handoff.json    # opens browser handoff URL
certifieddata verify ./bundle.zip                # offline bundle verification
```

The `generate` subcommand never uploads datasets. It opens a pre-filled web URL
carrying only aggregate counts (risk level, finding count, column count).

### @certifieddata/verify
Verify a CertifiedData certificate manifest using Ed25519 signature verification.
Supports: Node.js 18+, browser (Web Crypto API), Deno, Bun.

```ts
import { verifyManifest } from "@certifieddata/verify";
const result = await verifyManifest(envelope, publicKeyPem);
// { verified: true, alg: "Ed25519", key_id: "..." }
```

### @certifieddata/sdk
Convenience client that fetches manifests, signing keys, registry entries, and signed payloads.
Wraps @certifieddata/verify for full-stack verification in one call.

```ts
import { CertifiedDataClient } from "@certifieddata/sdk";
const client = new CertifiedDataClient("https://certifieddata.io");
const { manifest, result } = await client.fetchAndVerify(certId);
// Verify a local file hash against the certificate
const hashCheck = await client.verifyHash(certId, "sha256:abc123...");
```

### @certifieddata/schemas
TypeScript types and validators for certificate payloads.
Canonical source of schema version constants (cert.v1, cert.v2).

```ts
import { parseCertificatePayload, ACTIVE_SCHEMA_VERSION } from "@certifieddata/schemas";
const result = parseCertificatePayload(rawPayload);
// { ok: true, value: CertificatePayload } | { ok: false, reason: string }
```

### @certifieddata/embed
TypeScript module for rendering verification badge widgets.
Mirrors the embed.js script-tag integration as importable ES modules.

```ts
import { renderBadge } from "@certifieddata/embed";
await renderBadge(document.getElementById("badge"), { certId: "...", variant: "inline" });
```

### @certifieddata/badge-types
Shared TypeScript types for badge API responses and widget configuration.
Used by @certifieddata/embed and partner integrations.

## REST API

Base URL: https://certifieddata.io

### Certificate manifest (signed payload + signature)
GET /api/cert/:id/manifest
Accept: application/certifieddata.manifest+json
Returns the signed envelope for independent verification.

### Public signing keys
GET /.well-known/signing-keys.json
Returns active Ed25519 public keys used to sign certificates.

### Badge data
GET /api/badge/:id
Returns rich badge metadata: status, hashes, artifact name, verification URL.
CORS-enabled for cross-origin embed use.

### Badge analytics tracking
POST /api/badge/:id/track
Body: { event_type, source_url, install_type }
Fire-and-forget impression/click tracking for embedded badges.

### Certificate verification status
GET /verify/:id/status
Returns server-side verification result including revocation status.

Full OpenAPI spec: /openapi/openapi.yaml

## Verification flow

1. Fetch signed manifest from GET /api/cert/:id/manifest
2. Fetch public signing keys from GET /.well-known/signing-keys.json
3. Find the key matching signature.key_id
4. Assert key.status === "active"
5. Canonicalize payload: strip undefined, sort keys alphabetically, JSON.stringify
6. Verify Ed25519 signature over canonical payload bytes
7. Result: verified: true/false + reason if false

## Certificate schema

A signed certificate envelope looks like:

```json
{
  "schema_version": "certifieddata.manifest.v1",
  "payload": {
    "schema_version": "cert.v2",
    "certificate_id": "UUID",
    "certificate_type": "GENESIS",
    "issued_at": "ISO-8601",
    "issuer": {
      "name": "CertifiedData.io",
      "environment": "production",
      "signing_key_id": "key-id",
      "signature_alg": "Ed25519"
    },
    "subject": {
      "dataset_name": "...",
      "org_id": "...",
      "user_id": "..."
    },
    "hashes": {
      "certificate_payload_sha256": "hex",
      "datasets": {
        "filename.csv": {
          "sha256": "hex",
          "size_bytes": 12345,
          "row_count": 100000
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "signature": {
    "alg": "Ed25519",
    "key_id": "key-id",
    "value": "base64-encoded-signature"
  }
}
```

## Schema versions

- cert.v1 — initial schema (deprecated, still verifiable)
- cert.v2 — current schema (active)

## Examples

- examples/node-verify/ — Node.js verification using @certifieddata/verify
- examples/node-fetch-and-verify/ — SDK convenience wrapper
- examples/ci-verify/ — Standalone CI shell script, no npm install required
- examples/browser-verify/ — Zero-dependency HTML + Web Crypto API verification
- examples/python-verify/ — Python verification using cryptography library
- examples/embed-script/ — Script tag badge installation
- examples/embed-iframe/ — Iframe badge installation

## Links

- Platform: https://certifieddata.io
- Registry: https://certifieddata.io/registry
- API docs: https://certifieddata.io/docs/api
- OpenAPI: https://github.com/certifieddata/certifieddata-public/blob/main/openapi/openapi.yaml
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/org/certifieddata
